@Jaden Hercules. I was born in the 1980's so it wasn't nostalgic for me. As you know anything before you're born seems ancient. So the 1960's might as well be the Wild West or Roman times to me. I enjoyed it because of its depth. Because it was about the meaning of everything it means to be human. For me it made me think about the meaning of life, the meaning of friendship, self identity, and how we deal with evolving times and changes in our lives that can challenge the most important things to us. The way the characters were written and acted was perfect. It also had a theme of how Hollywood influences life and how life influences Hollywood. It's one of the deepest and most perfectly executed films of all time.
